topic: An artist's work is displayed without their permission
raw_output:
[story 1]

The gallery lights felt too bright, a harsh spotlight dissecting the canvas I’d poured my soul into. I stood near the entrance, my hands twisting the frayed cuff of my worn sweater, watching people glide past the framed pieces. Each glance they spared my work felt like a brief, cold brush against my skin. I kept my gaze fixed on the polished floor, the silence of the room amplifying the hollow echo in my chest. A woman paused, tilting her head, a flicker of interest crossing her face before she moved on. I felt a tightening in my throat, a desperate urge to dissolve into the cool marble beneath my feet. This vast, echoing space seemed to swallow any sound I tried to make.

[story 2]

I traced the outline of my latest sculpture with a fingertip, the cool, unyielding metal offering no warmth. The murmur of conversation around me was a distant, unintelligible tide. I watched a group of patrons cluster around a vibrant abstract piece, their laughter sharp and bright. My shoulders slumped almost imperceptibly, a physical weight settling onto my spine. *No one sees it,* I thought, the realization a dull ache behind my eyes. I shifted my weight, the movement stiff, and retreated toward a shadowed alcove, seeking the anonymity of the darkened corner. The air here smelled faintly of dust and old varnish, a familiar, muted comfort.

[story 3]

The velvet rope felt impossibly far away. I stood by the exit, clutching a lukewarm coffee cup, the steam doing little to warm the chill that had settled deep in my bones. The sheer volume of admiring eyes directed at the exhibited art felt like a physical pressure, an unwanted scrutiny. I kept glancing back at the canvases—my vulnerability laid bare, uninvited. My breath hitched slightly, a shallow, quick intake of air. I started walking, my steps heavy and slow, each movement an effort against the inertia of the quiet room. The street outside was too loud, too full of hurried connections I couldn't reach.
